selection.greatFrontendReasonIfOmitted: No exact-topic GreatFrontEnd page was confirmed for this JavaScript prompt, so broader JavaScript question-bank pages would be weaker competitors than topic-direct sources.

competitors.label: Using Web Workers
competitors.selectionReason: MDN is the canonical baseline for Web Worker lifecycle, messaging, and structured-clone behavior.
theirStrengths: Using Web Workers is a direct, credible comparator for this topic.
theirGaps: Using Web Workers is less compressed around the specific interview framing and likely follow-up confusion this page targets.

realWorldUseCases: What is a Web Worker? A Web Worker runs JavaScript on a separate thread from the main UI thread. Use it for CPU-heavy work (parsing, compression, image processing) so the UI stays responsive and scrolling stays smooth.
actionableExamples: Real-world scenario Example: you need to parse a 50MB JSON export and compute stats while the user keeps scrolling. Doing this on the main thread will jank the UI. Offload the parsing + aggregation to a worker, then send the small result back to the UI thread.
followUpConfusion: What is a Web Worker? A Web Worker runs JavaScript on a separate thread from the main UI thread. Use it for CPU-heavy work (parsing, compression, image processing) so the UI stays responsive and scrolling stays smooth.

realWorldUseCases: Using Web Workers covers the same topic directly, but our shipped answer is more explicit about the production or interview decision boundary.
actionableExamples: Using Web Workers includes a credible baseline explanation or example, so the example criterion stays tied.
followUpConfusion: Using Web Workers is less direct on the likely follow-up confusion than our shipped answer.
